topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The laptop screen glared back at me, illuminating the frantic scribbles of prose. I had meant to look over the shared document for grocery lists, but instead, my eyes snagged on a chapter titled, "The Unseen Observer." My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake of air that seemed to catch in my throat. It was *my* life, laid out in meticulously crafted sentences, a tapestry woven entirely from my mundane Tuesday evenings. A tremor started in my hands, making the mouse dance erratically across the trackpad. I leaned closer, my vision blurring slightly, trying to reconcile the elegant, dramatic prose with the reality of my partner—the person who made me coffee and forgot to take out the recycling. A hot flush crept up my neck, turning my skin prickly. "What... what is this?" The sound of my own voice was thin, reedy, completely unlike the steady tone I usually employed. I slammed the laptop shut, the resulting thud echoing loudly in the quiet apartment, and backed away until the cool plaster of the wall pressed against my spine.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked beneath a stack of well-worn poetry books—a manuscript, bound in soft, cream-colored paper. My heart began to hammer against my ribs, a frantic drum solo against my sternum. Every word swam before my eyes: descriptions of my morning routine, the precise shade of my favorite sweater, the slight, almost imperceptible sigh I let out when I was truly tired. I picked up a page, and the ink seemed to vibrate under my touch. A strange, dizzying wave washed over me, making the room tilt. I started pacing the small living room, my movements jerky and uncontrolled. "You... you wrote this?" I managed to ask, the words coming out as a breathless rush. My palms were slick against the cover of the book. I couldn't seem to settle; I kept running my fingers through my hair, pulling at the strands as if to physically pull the impossible situation apart. The silence of the room suddenly felt deafening, amplifying the frantic thumping in my ears.

[story 3]

It was the footnotes that finally broke me. Hidden within the margins of a passage describing a shared walk through the park, my partner had detailed my exact reaction to a particularly bright patch of sunlight. The realization hit me with the force of a physical blow, stealing the air from my lungs. I stood frozen by the doorway, my body rigid, every muscle tense. A strange, bubbling sensation rose in my chest, a chaotic surge that made my vision swim with uncontrolled light. I dropped the coffee mug I was holding; it shattered on the hardwood floor, the sound insignificant compared to the internal cacophony raging within me. I could only stare at the manuscript, my mouth dry and refusing to form coherent sounds. A low, shaky sound escaped my throat—a pathetic little whimper—and I pressed the heels of my hands against my eyes, trying desperately to force the image away, but it was already burned onto my retina.
